hero.title: 4 Days: Best of Siem Reap
hero.titleAccent: Angkor Temples + Tonle Sap Lake Cruise.
hero.intro: Our most popular Cambodia package. Four days that cover everything Siem Reap is famous for: a full-day Angkor temple circuit with Angkor Wat, Angkor Thom and Ta Prohm, a morning boat cruise on Tonle Sap Lake through floating villages and migratory bird habitats, and enough time to explore the old quarter, Pub Street and the Angkor Night Market at a relaxed pace.

breadcrumb.label: 4 Days Best of Siem Reap & Tonle Sap

hero.heroImageAlt: Tonle Sap Lake floating village boat cruise Cambodia Siem Reap

includes: Meals as per itinerary (B = Breakfast, L = Lunch, D = Dinner)
All tours and sightseeing as per itinerary
excludes: Travel insurance (ETIQA Area 2 Gold from RM41)

overview.heading: 4 Days Cambodia: The Full Picture
body: This is Dhesu's best-selling Cambodia package and the one we recommend most often for first-time visitors from Malaysia. Four days gives you everything Siem Reap has to offer without the compressed feeling of a three-day itinerary: time to explore the city properly on arrival, a full unrushed day at the Angkor temples, and an entire morning on Tonle Sap Lake.

The Angkor temple circuit is the reason most people come to Cambodia, and Day 2 covers it completely: Angkor Wat at its most photogenic, the great walled city of Angkor Thom with its 200-faced Bayon, Ta Prohm with its tree roots and Tomb Raider filming locations, and Pre Rup for the most celebrated sunset in Asia.

Day 3 adds what makes this package different from the basic three-day version: Tonle Sap Lake. Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake lies 15 kilometres south of Siem Reap and fills dramatically each monsoon season. The boat cruise through Chong Kneas into the floating village communities — floating schools, floating churches, families living their entire lives on the water — is one of the most memorable experiences Cambodia offers. The afternoon continues with Artisan D'Angkor, where traditional Khmer craftsmanship in silk, lacquer, silver and stone is preserved and demonstrated.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day Cambodia Package Stands Apart

items.title: Angkor Wat & The Full Temple Circuit
items.body: The world's largest religious monument in a full unrushed day. Angkor Wat at sunrise, Angkor Thom's 54-towered Bayon, the jungle-reclaimed Ta Prohm, and the Pre Rup sunset pyramid. Your guide explains not just what you're seeing but why it was built, what happened to it, and why it survived.
items.imageAlt: Angkor Wat temple sunrise Cambodia reflection

items.title: Tonle Sap Lake — Southeast Asia's Largest Freshwater Lake
items.body: A morning boat cruise from Chong Kneas into a world most tourists never see. Floating villages of 3,000-5,000 people where houses, schools, churches and markets all sit on the water. The lake rises 9 metres every monsoon season, forcing entire communities to migrate. The wildlife — migratory waterbirds, freshwater dolphins, endemic species — is equally remarkable. Nothing quite like this exists elsewhere in Southeast Asia.
items.imageAlt: Tonle Sap Lake floating village boat cruise Cambodia

items.title: Artisan D'Angkor — Khmer Craftsmanship Preserved
items.body: Established in 1992 to preserve traditional Khmer arts threatened with extinction, Artisan D'Angkor employs over 1,000 craftspeople working in silk weaving, lacquerware, stone and wood carving, and silver jewellery. The workshop tour shows artisans at work on the same techniques used to build and decorate Angkor's temples. The attached showroom is the best place in Cambodia to buy quality handmade souvenirs.
items.imageAlt: Artisan D'Angkor silk weaving traditional Khmer crafts Siem Reap

items.title: Traditional Khmer Dance Dinner
items.body: An evening cultural performance of traditional Apsara dance — the sacred dance of the Khmer kingdom depicted in hundreds of bas-reliefs across the Angkor temples. Performed by trained dancers in elaborate classical costume to the music of traditional instruments, this is one of Cambodia's most distinctive cultural experiences. Combined with a Khmer buffet dinner, it is the ideal close to the Angkor day.
items.imageAlt: Traditional Khmer Apsara dance performance Cambodia cultural show

days.title: Arrival Siem Reap — City Discovery Tour
days.description: Arrive at Siem Reap International Airport and transfer to hotel. Full afternoon city discovery tour: Wat Preah Prom Rath (the most beautiful temple in Siem Reap city), the twin Preah Ang Chek and Preah Ang Chorm Shrines (the Sisters Temple), Royal Gardens, Wat Thmei Killing Field Memorial, and the War Memorial Monument. Visit the Satcha Handicraft Centre. Dinner included. Evening at leisure to explore Pub Street and Angkor Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Siem Reap airport arrival & hotel transfer
Preah Ang Chek & Preah Ang Chorm Sisters Temple

days.title: Full Day Angkor Archaeological Circuit | Traditional Khmer Dance Dinner
days.description: Full day at the Angkor Archaeological Park — the main circuit covering all the essential temples. Morning: Angkor Wat at sunrise, the most photographed monument in Southeast Asia. The 350-metre causeway approach, the outer gallery bas-reliefs, and the five central towers. Angkor Thom via the South Gate causeway of 108 stone figures; the Bayon with its 200 carved faces; Baphuon Temple pyramid; the Terrace of Elephants and Terrace of the Leper King. Afternoon: Ta Prohm (the Tomb Raider temple), Banteay Kdei monastery, Srah Srang royal bathing reservoir, and Pre Rup sunset pyramid. Evening: Traditional Khmer Apsara dance performance with buffet dinner (subject to availability). Pub Street and Angkor Night Market after dinner.
suggestedActivities: Angkor Thom South Gate & Bayon Temple
Terrace of Elephants & Terrace of Leper King

days.title: Tonle Sap Lake Cruise | Cultural Tour | Pub Street
days.description: Morning boat cruise on Tonle Sap Lake from Chong Kneas — Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake and one of the most extraordinary inland water environments on earth. Float through the floating villages of Chong Kneas: floating houses, floating schools, floating petrol stations. Watch fishermen casting nets from dugout canoes and migratory waterbirds feeding in the shallows. Return to Siem Reap for lunch. Afternoon: Old Market (Psar Chas) browsing, then Artisan D'Angkor Centre where Khmer craftspeople work on traditional silk weaving, lacquerware and stone carving. Visit Angkor Green Garden Wax Museum. Evening stroll through Pub Street and Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Tonle Sap Lake boat cruise from Chong Kneas

days.title: Morning at Leisure — Depart Siem Reap
days.description: Morning at leisure for personal activities or last-minute shopping. The Old Market, Angkor Night Market, and the streets around the Pub Street area all have good souvenir shops for silk, silver, handmade crafts and Cambodian coffee. Transfer to Siem Reap International Airport for onward journey.

whyBook.heading: Cambodia Made Easy — By the Agency That Has Done It Since 1988
whyBook.body: The best-selling Cambodia package in our range for good reason: it covers everything that matters about Siem Reap without leaving anything important out. Four days is the right length. Tonle Sap Lake is not optional — it is one of the defining experiences of any Cambodia trip. Dhesu has been arranging this journey for Malaysian travellers since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We handle Cambodia visa documentation guidance, Angkor pass arrangements, and all ground logistics.

points.title: 35+ Years of Cambodia Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to Cambodia. Our guides know the temples well and the lake even better.

points.title: Daily Departures, No Waiting
points.body: Your trip runs on your chosen dates with your own private guide and vehicle. No group minimums. No sharing with strangers.

points.title: Muslim-Friendly Options
points.body: Halal meals arranged at no extra cost. Masjid AN NEAKMAH in Siem Reap included on request. All dietary requirements accommodated.

sections.heading: 4 Day Cambodia Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 4-day Cambodia tour from Malaysia is the ideal first visit: long enough to see the full Angkor circuit without rushing, and to include Tonle Sap Lake — which many first-time visitors are surprised to discover is as memorable as the temples. Four days is the most popular duration for Malaysian travellers visiting Cambodia, and this package covers everything that makes Siem Reap worth the journey.

The combination of the Angkor temple circuit on Day 2 and the Tonle Sap Lake cruise on Day 3 is why this is consistently our best-selling Cambodia package. Each experience is extraordinary on its own terms. Together they give you a complete picture of what Cambodia is — ancient stone kingdom and living water world.

sections.heading: Tonle Sap Lake: What to Expect
sections.body:
Tonle Sap Lake is Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake and one of the most productive inland fisheries on earth. The lake is fed by the Mekong River and reverses its flow each year: in the wet season (June to October) water flows from the Mekong into the lake, expanding it from 2,500 to 16,000 square kilometres. In the dry season the flow reverses, draining back into the Mekong and concentrating the fish catch.

The floating villages that cluster around the lake's edges are permanent communities that migrate seasonally with the water levels. A boat cruise from Chong Kneas takes you through these communities — floating schools, floating churches, floating petrol stations and floating restaurants. Children paddle dugout canoes alongside your boat. It is unlike anything else in the region.

sections.heading: Best Hotels in Siem Reap for This Package
sections.body:
Siem Reap's hotel district clusters around the Siem Reap River, within easy reach of the Old Market and a short drive from the Angkor Archaeological Park entrance. The 3-star options (Holy Angkor, Kouprey) are clean, comfortable and well-located. The 4-star options (Somadevi, Angkor Holiday) add pool access and more refined dining. The 5-star Angkor Miracle Resort offers the best views and facilities in the city.

For most first-time visitors, the 4-star option at RM1,688 per person represents the best value — good facilities without the premium of the 5-star tier, and the location is just as convenient.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Cambodia?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders require a visa to enter Cambodia. Options include the Cambodia e-Visa (apply online before departure, approximately USD 36, processed in 3 business days) or visa on arrival at Siem Reap airport (approximately USD 30, cash only). Dhesu provides pre-departure guidance on the visa process for every booking.

items.question: When is the best time to visit Tonle Sap Lake?
items.answer: The floating villages are accessible year-round. The lake is at its fullest from August to November, when the villages are most extensive and the boat journey most impressive. From December to May the water level drops and the floating community moves closer to the main water channels. Either way, the experience is extraordinary. November to February is the most comfortable weather for the boat cruise.

items.question: Is the Khmer dance dinner guaranteed?
items.answer: The traditional Khmer Apsara dance buffet dinner is subject to availability and seasonal scheduling. When it is available, it is included in the Day 2 evening as listed. During periods when performances are not scheduled, dinner is instead at a local restaurant with traditional Khmer cuisine. Your guide will confirm the current schedule before the tour.

items.question: What is Artisan D'Angkor?
items.answer: Artisan D'Angkor is a social enterprise established in 1992 to preserve traditional Khmer arts. It employs over 1,000 craftspeople across Cambodia working in silk weaving, lacquerware, stone carving, wood carving and silver jewellery — the same traditional techniques used to build and decorate Angkor's temples. The workshop in Siem Reap allows visitors to watch artisans at work. It is also the best source for quality, ethically-made Cambodian souvenirs.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: This is a 100% private tour. You have your own licensed English-speaking guide and private vehicle for all 4 days. The itinerary follows your own pace. Dhesu does not run group departures for Cambodia.

items.question: Can I arrange halal food throughout the tour?
items.answer: Yes. Halal meals can be arranged throughout your stay in Siem Reap at no extra cost. Siem Reap has several good halal restaurants. Masjid AN NEAKMAH in Siem Reap can be included in your Day 1 or Day 3 itinerary on request.
